description: federal city and former capital of Russia
description_wiki: Saint Petersburg, formerly known as Petrograd (Петроград) and later Leningrad (Ленинград), is the second-largest city in Russia, after Moscow, the nation's capital.

description: federal city and former capital of Russia
description_wiki: Saint Petersburg, formerly known as Petrograd (Петроград) and later Leningrad (Ленинград), is the second-largest city in Russia, after Moscow, the nation's capital.

aliases: Sovereign, Tsar and Grand Prince of all Russia
description: the royal title of the rulers of Tsardom of Russia from 1547 to 1721
imperial Russian military unit
description_wiki: The Chevalier Guard Regiment (Russian: Кавалергардский полк, romanized: Kavalergardskiy polk) was a Russian heavy cavalry guard regiment, created in 1800 by the reformation of the Chevalier Guard corps, itself created in 1764 by Catherine the Great.

description: city in Wayne County, Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Grosse Pointe is a city in Wayne County in the U.S. state of Michigan.

surface_forms: state Senator George Van Ness Lothrop
description: American politician (1817-1897)
country in Northern Europe
description_wiki: Estonia, officially the Republic of Estonia, is a country in the Baltic region of Northern Europe.

description: river in Russia connecting Lake Ladoga and the Baltic Sea

entity: St. Peter and St. Paul Fortress
surface_forms: fortress of St. Peter and St. Paul
description: original citadel of St. Petersburg, Russia
description_wiki: The Peter and Paul Fortress (Russian: Петропавловская крепость, romanized: Petropavlovskaya krepost') is the original citadel of Saint Petersburg, Russia, founded by Peter the Great in 1703 and built to Domenico Trezzini's designs from 1706 to 1740 as a star fortress.

description: sea between Europe and Asia
description_wiki: The Black Sea is a marginal sea lying between Europe and Asia, east of the Balkans, south of the East European Plain, west of the Caucasus, and north of Anatolia.

description: Mediterranean coastline of the southeast corner of France
description_wiki: The French Riviera, known in French as the Côte d'Azur (French: [kot dazyʁ] ; Provençal: Còsta d'Azur, pronounced [ˈkwɔstɔ daˈzyʀ]; lit. 'Azure Coast'), is the Mediterranean coastline of the southeast corner of France.

description: country in southern Europe
description_wiki: Italy, officially the Italian Republic, is a country in Southern and Western Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: Russian poet, playwright, and novelist (1799–1837)
description_wiki: Alexander Sergeyevich Pushkin (6 June [O.S. 26 May] 1799 – 10 February [O.S. 29 January] 1837) was a Russian poet, playwright, and novelist of the Romantic era.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: Austro-Hungarian politician (1863-1942)
description_wiki: Leopold Anton Johann Sigismund Josef Korsinus Ferdinand Graf Berchtold von und zu Ungarschitz, Frättling und Püllütz (Hungarian: Gróf Berchtold Lipót, Czech: Leopold hrabě Berchtold z Uherčic) (18 April 1863 – 21 November 1942) was an Austro-Hungarian politician, diplomat and statesman who served as Imperial Foreign Minister at the outbreak of World War I.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Austria, formally the Republic of Austria, is a landlocked country in Central Europe, lying in the Eastern Alps.

description: city in Crimea, Ukraine
description_wiki: Yalta (Russian and Ukrainian: Ялта) is a resort city on the south coast of the Crimean Peninsula surrounded by the Black Sea.

description: Russian mystic (1869–1916)
description_wiki: Grigori Yefimovich Rasputin (21 January [O.S. 9 January] 1869 – 30 December [O.S. 17 December] 1916) was a Russian mystic and faith healer.

description: Russian prince
description_wiki: Knyaz Felix Felixovich Yusupov, Count Sumarokov-Elston (Russian: Князь Фе́ликс Фе́ликсович Юсу́пов, Граф Сумаро́ков-Эльстон; 23 March [O.S. 11 March] 1887 – 27 September 1967) was a Russian aristocrat from the House of Yusupov who is best known for participating in the assassination of Grigori Rasputin and for marrying Princess Irina Alexandrovna, a niece of Emperor Nicholas II.

description: 1917-1922 political change in Russia
description_wiki: The Russian Revolution was a period of political and social change in Russia, starting in 1917.

entity: Queen Alexandra of the United Kingdom
aliases: Alexandra Caroline Marie Charlotte Louise Julia
Alexandra, Queen of the United Kingdom
Princess Alexandra Caroline Marie Charlotte Louise Julia of Denmark
Princess Alexandra Caroline Marie Charlotte Louise Julia of Schleswig-Holstein-Sonderburg-Glücksburg
Alexandra of Denmark, Queen of the United Kingdom
Queen Alexandra of the United Kingdom
description: Queen consort of the United Kingdom from 1901 to 1910 (1844–1925)
description_wiki:
Alexandra of Denmark (Alexandra Caroline Marie Charlotte Louise Julia; 1 December 1844 – 20 November 1925) was Queen of the United Kingdom and the British Dominions, and Empress of India, from 22 January 1901 to 6 May 1910 as the wife of King Edward VII.
Alexandra's family had been relatively obscure until 1852, when her father, Prince Christian of Schleswig-Holstein-Sonderburg-Glücksburg, was chosen (with the consent of the major European powers) to succeed his second cousin Frederick VII as King of Denmark.

description: palace on the Crimea
description_wiki: Livadia Palace (Russian: Ливадийский дворец; Ukrainian: Лівадійський палац) is a former summer retreat of the last Russian tsar, Nicholas II, and his family in Livadiya, Crimea.

description: port city in Crimea, Ukraine
description_wiki: Sevastopol ( SEV-ə-STOH-pəl, sə-VAS-tə-pohl), sometimes written Sebastopol, is the largest city in Crimea and a major port on the Black Sea.

description: noble title with several historical meanings
description_wiki: Sultan (; Arabic: سلطان sulṭān, pronounced [sʊlˈtˤɑːn, solˈtˤɑːn]) is a position with several historical meanings.

description: faction of the Marxist Russian Social Democratic Labour Party; a major faction in the Russian Civil War
description_wiki: The Bolsheviks, led by Vladimir Lenin, were a radical faction of the Marxist Russian Social Democratic Labour Party (RSDLP) which split with the Mensheviks at the Second Party Congress in 1903.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: Russian politician, prime minister in 1917 (1881–1970)
description_wiki: Alexander Fyodorovich Kerensky (4 May [O.S. 22 April] 1881 – 11 June 1970) was a Russian politician, lawyer and revolutionary who led the Russian Provisional Government and the short-lived Russian Republic for three months from late July to early November 1917 (N.S.).

description: country in Northern Europe
description_wiki: Finland, or the Republic of Finland, is a Nordic country in Northern Europe.

description: country in Northern Europe
description_wiki: Sweden, formally the Kingdom of Sweden, is a Nordic country located on the Scandinavian Peninsula in Northern Europe.

description: country in Northern Europe
description_wiki: Norway, officially the Kingdom of Norway, is a Nordic country comprising the western and northernmost parts of the Scandinavian Peninsula in Northern Europe, the remote Arctic island Jan Mayen and the archipelago Svalbard.

description: United Kingdom military land warfare branch of the British Armed Forces
description_wiki: The British Army is the land warfare force of the United Kingdom responsible for defending the UK, the British Overseas Territories and Crown Dependencies.

aliases: Her Majesty's Royal Palace and Fortress
His Majesty's Royal Palace and Fortress
description: castle in central London, United Kingdom
description_wiki: The Tower of London, officially His Majesty's Royal Palace and Fortress of the Tower of London, is a historic citadel and castle on the north bank of the River Thames in London, England.

description: city in the northwest of Russia
description_wiki: Arkhangelsk (Russian: Архангельск, pronounced [ɐrˈxanɡʲɪlʲsk]) is a city and the administrative center of Arkhangelsk Oblast, Russia.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: city in Macedonia, Greece
description_wiki: Thessaloniki (; Greek: Θεσσαλονίκη [θesaloˈnici] ; also known by various spellings and names) is a city in northern Greece.

description: geographical and historical region in southeastern Europe, today forming parts of the Republics of Greece, North Macedonia and Bulgaria
description_wiki: Macedonia (  MASS-ih-DOH-nee-ə) is a geographical and historical region of the Balkan Peninsula in Southeast Europe.

description: largest city in Turkey
description_wiki: Istanbul is the largest city in Turkey, constituting the country's economic, cultural, and historical center.

description: part of Russia
description_wiki: Southern Russia or the South of Russia (Russian: Юг России, IPA: [juk rɐˈsʲiɪ]) is a colloquial term for the southernmost geographic portion of European Russia.

description: capital of Krasnodar region (Krai) in Southern Russia
description_wiki: Krasnodar, formerly Yekaterinodar (until 1920), is the largest city and the administrative centre of Krasnodar Krai, Russia.

description: port city in Rostov Oblast in southern Russia
description_wiki: Taganrog (Russian: Таганрог, IPA: [təɡɐnˈrok]) is a port city in Rostov Oblast, Russia, on the north shore of Taganrog Bay in the Sea of Azov, several kilometers west of the mouth of the Don River.

description: Inland sea of ​​the Atlantic Ocean basin
description_wiki: The Sea of Azov is an inland shelf sea in Eastern Europe connected to the Black Sea by the narrow (about 4 km (2.5 mi)) Strait of Kerch, and sometimes regarded as a northern extension of the Black Sea.

surface_forms: brigand by the name of Makhno
description: Ukrainian anarchist revolutionary (1888–1934)
description_wiki: Nestor Ivanovych Makhno (Ukrainian: Нестор Іванович Махно, pronounced [ˈnɛstor iˈwɑnowɪtʃ mɐxˈnɔ]; 7 November 1888 – 25 July 1934), also known as Bat'ko Makhno (Ukrainian: Батько Махно [ˈbɑtʲko mɐxˈnɔ], lit. 'Father Makhno'), was a Ukrainian anarchist revolutionary and the commander of the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine during the Ukrainian War of Independence.

description: 5th-century ruler of the Hunnic Empire
city and administrative center of Volgograd Oblast in southern Russia
1942 major battle of World War II
description_wiki: The Battle of Stalingrad (17 July 1942 – 2 February 1943) was a major battle on the Eastern Front of World War II, beginning when Nazi Germany and its Axis allies attacked and became locked in a protracted struggle with the Soviet Union for control over the Soviet city of Stalingrad (now known as Volgograd) in southern Russia.

description: city and administrative center of Volgograd Oblast in southern Russia
description_wiki: Volgograd, formerly Tsaritsyn (1589–1925) and Stalingrad (1925–1961), is the largest city and the administrative centre of Volgograd Oblast, Russia.

description: river in Russia; the longest river in Europe
largest landlocked salt lake, located between Europe and Asia
description_wiki: The Caspian Sea is the world's largest inland body of water, described as the world's largest lake and usually referred to as a full-fledged sea.

description: city in the south of Russia
description_wiki: Astrakhan (Russian: Астрахань, IPA: [ˈastrəxənʲ] ) is the largest city and administrative centre of Astrakhan Oblast in southern Russia.

description: leader of the Soviet Union from 1924 to 1953
description_wiki: Joseph Vissarionovich Stalin (né Dzhugashvili; 18 December [O.S. 6 December] 1878 – 5 March 1953) was a Soviet revolutionary and politician who led the Soviet Union from 1924 until his death in 1953.

description: group of infectious diseases
description_wiki: Typhus, also known as typhus fever, is a group of infectious diseases that include epidemic typhus, scrub typhus, and murine typhus.

description: Nazi concentration camp in Thuringia (1937–1945)
description_wiki: Buchenwald (German pronunciation: [ˈbuːxn̩valt]; 'beech forest') was a German Nazi concentration camp established on Ettersberg hill near Weimar, Germany, in July 1937.

description: Russian army general of Baltic German origin (1878-1928)
description_wiki: Baron Pyotr Nikolayevich Wrangel (Russian: Пётр Николаевич Врангель [ˈpʲɵtr nʲɪkɐˈlajɪvʲɪtɕ ˈvranɡʲɪlʲ]; August 27 [O.S. August 15] 1878 – 25 April 1928) was a Russian military officer of Baltic German descent.

description: major faction in the Russian Civil War
description_wiki: The White movement, also known as the Whites, was one of the main factions of the Russian Civil War of 1917–1922.
